---
title: "Income Inequality and Happiness in Europe"
subtitle: "Data collection and descriptive analysis workflow"
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: simple
    slide-number: true
execute:
  echo: true
  warning: false
  message: false
---

## Project process

1. Collect **macro-level** indicators from Eurostat (SDMX 3.0 API)
2. Collect **micro-level** public happiness data from ESS
3. Merge data on country codes and year for all European countries
4. Produce descriptive summaries and visualisations
5. Extend with modelling once model choices are finalized

## Data collection scripts

- `scripts/01_collect_eurostat_macro.R` (Eurostat API)
- `scripts/02_collect_ess_micro.R` (ESS API)
- `scripts/03_merge_micro_macro.R` (merge micro + macro)
- `scripts/04_analysis_outline.R` (descriptives and plot)
- `scripts/00_master.R` (run all scripts)

## Descriptive results

```{r}
library(readr)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)

df <- read_csv("data/processed/analysis_dataset.csv", show_col_types = FALSE)

desc <- df |>
  group_by(country_code, year) |>
  summarise(
    mean_happiness = mean(happiness, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_gini = mean(gini, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

ggplot(desc, aes(mean_gini, mean_happiness, color = country_code)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE, color = "black") +
  labs(
    title = "Descriptive pattern: inequality and happiness",
    x = "Gini coefficient",
    y = "Mean happiness"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```

## Next steps

- Finalize model strategy
- Estimate and compare selected models
- Add robustness checks and interpretative slides
